claims: PEP 659 calls specialization the most important optimization for dynamic Python, enabled by adaptive instructions that replace themselves with specialized variants at runtime.
Inline caches store per-instruction metadata (offsets, type info); counters trigger de-specialization back to adaptive form when assumptions fail.
Specialization targets attribute lookup, globals, and calls, with claimed 10-60% speedups and measurable memory cost per instruction.
Dynamic-language VMs must specialize code to observed types/values to perform well, with rapid deoptimization as the robustness mechanism.
caveats: Informational PEP; implementation details evolve, and no formal benchmark data is included in the PEP itself.

claims: V8 tracks ~21 element kinds per array and generates type-specific code for small-integer, double, and general element arrays.
Element-kind transitions are one-directional from specific to general; holey arrays generally stay holey and need extra prototype-chain checks.
Monomorphic operations optimize better; polymorphic element handling triggers extra checks and possible deoptimization.
caveats: Blog-level source; real-world packed/holey differences are often negligible per the source itself.

claims: V8 objects carry a map (hidden class) pointer enabling O(1) shape comparison; property addition creates transition chains between maps.
TurboFan embeds constant field values into optimized code and marks dependent code for deoptimization when such fields mutate.
Optimized code verifies map identity so layout assumptions stay valid; out-of-order property addition bifurcates transition chains.
caveats: Docs say the subject is rich and subject to change; inline caches referenced but not detailed on this page.

claims: PyPy uses a tracing JIT that traces the RPython interpreter rather than the user program, making the JIT language-implementation-generic.
The JIT pipeline is tracer, optimizer over residual operations, and machine-code backend, driven by hints in the interpreter.
caveats: Architecture page does not detail guards/hot-loop thresholds; deeper claims need PyPy papers.

claims: Tiered compilation uses the client compiler during profiling so the server compiler final code arrives with faster startup, at the cost of a much larger code cache.
HotSpot escape analysis classifies allocations (NoEscape/ArgEscape/GlobalEscape), eliminating scalar-replaceable allocations and eliding locks on thread-local objects.
Oracle documentation states escape analysis does not replace heap allocation with stack allocation; it only eliminates allocations and locks.
Compressed oops trade decode/encode overhead for pointer-size reduction, showing runtime data-representation choices affect performance.
caveats: Version-specific documentation; speculation/deoptimization internals are not covered by this page.
